title: Light Show-off Argentinian Chicken Salad
description: A rich and light dish featuring a blend of cumin-spiced chicken with raw-friendly vegetables for a show-off Argentinean meal.

ingredients.name: Jalapeno Pepper
ingredients.amount: 1 small, seeded and finely chopped (adjust for heat preference)

instructions: In a mixing bowl, combine minced garlic and cumin powder. Season with salt and pepper.
Cut the chicken into bite-sized pieces. Add them to the spice mixture, toss well, and marinate for at least 20 minutes or up to several hours in the refrigerator.
Heat olive oil in a non-stick skillet over medium-high heat. Remove chicken from marinade and discard excess liquid. Sear the chicken until golden brown on all sides, about 5-7 minutes per side.
Remove chicken from skillet and set aside. Pour out any remaining fat from the skillet and wipe clean or add new oil if needed.
Add red onion and jalapeno to the skillet. Cook for another minute before adding cilantro. Stir in lime juice. Sauté until onions become translucent, about 2-3 minutes more.
Return chicken to the skillet with the vegetables, toss gently. Remove from heat.
Divide lettuce leaves on plates or bowls. Spoon chicken mixture over the top. Drizzle with extra lime juice and additional olive oil if desired.
